sections.paragraph:
Φορτουνᾶτος, ου, ὁ (also Φουρ-; Lat. Fortunatus. OGI 707, 5 Φορτουνᾶτος Σεβαστοῦ ἀπελεύθερος; APF 2, 1903, 571 no. 151, 5; Jos., Ant. 18, 247. S. Lghtf., The Apost. Fathers, Pt. I, vol. I, 1890 p. 29, 3; 62, 1, exx. fr. Lat. sources) Fortunatus.

① an otherw. unknown Christian of Corinth who, w. his Christian fellow-townsmen Stephanas and Achaicus, was w. Paul in Ephesus when 1 Cor was written, 1 Cor 16:15 v.l., 17; subscr.

② a member of the delegation sent by the Roman church to Corinth 1 Cl 65:1.—DDD 637. M-M.

Lat. Lat. = Latin

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

vol. vol. = volume

exx. exx. = examples

  1. fr. = from

otherw. otherw. = otherwise

subscr. subscr. = subscription

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DDD DDD = Dictionary of Deities and Demons in the Bible—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1064
